rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no monetary columns present for this line.

rows.description: Item To Dr Levett for impensa and Nota Domr Nat. 1600
rows.notes: Amounts (23.1.10) are clearly aligned in the rightmost three columns; handwriting and column alignment unambiguous.

rows.description: Item to Dr Pain for Greeneways fine 15s 6d Pens fine 1.1.8
rows.notes: Right column shows '0 17 2' (0 pounds, 17s, 2d). The description contains multiple fines; chosen combined amount 17s 2d based on the single numeric group aligned with this line. Minor uncertainty in reading 'Pain/Pain' and exact punctuation.
Latin header for the following group of receipts/payments. No amounts directly on this line; text is legible.

rows.description: Ad festum Nativ. p aucam. Southn. et Merton — Stipend. et Officariijs
rows.notes: Amount 22.15.0 clearly aligned with this line; column boundaries unambiguous. Handwritten abbreviations in description are legible enough to preserve exact text.

rows.description: Ad Fest. Ann. p augusto Red. de Cudl. 3 p[?]
rows.notes: Amount 17.0.0 is clearly aligned. Some abbreviations in the description (e.g. '3 p[?]') are slightly unclear; chosen literal transcription and assigned moderate confidence.

Single-word line 'Stipend' appears with amount 38.1.9 in the numeric column. The association (whether this is a sub-item or its own entry) is slightly ambiguous; reading of numbers is reasonably clear but the exact linkage to preceding line is uncertain.

rows.description: Ad Fest. Bapt. p 4th pre Augm. Red. de Cudl.
rows.notes: Amount 28.0.0 aligned with this line. Handwritten abbreviations of festival and place are legible though archaic; assignment of the amount to this description is based on top-to-bottom alignment.

Amount 12.10.8 visible in the numeric column next to this line. The phrase appears to be a continuation/clarification; still treated as an 'entry' per instructions because it is a transaction line with its own amount.

rows.description: Ad Fest. Mich p aug. Red. de Longwitn.
rows.notes: Amount 17.0.0 is aligned to this line. Text abbreviation 'Longwitn.' read as 'Longwitn' (likely Longwitton/Longwitn); moderate confidence due to abbreviation.
Numeric cluster 22.16.1 is legible and aligns with this short line 'Solut. p Guineas'. 'Solut.' interpreted as 'soluto/soluta' or payment; text is abbreviated but amount reading is clear.

rows.description: To Dr Levett for mr vivians Butts { Mich 99.2.4 Nativ.99.2.8 }
rows.notes: Assigned amount 22.5.0 to this line following the vertical order of numeric groups. The description is cramped and contains a brace with two internal figures; it's ambiguous whether the amount is a consolidation or related specifically to one sub-item. Chosen the single numeric group next in order.

rows.description: To mr Harrys for mr Tivians Batts { Mich 98.2.4 Nativ. 98.2.7 }
rows.notes: Next numeric group reads 0.5.0 (0 pounds, 5s, 0d). The name 'Harrys' and 'Tivians' are read from script; there is some ambiguity in proper names and braces. Amount reading is clear but the linkage between the brace details and the numeric total is moderately uncertain.

rows.description: For timber for carrying on ye New buildings; ye money being given (as appears in ye last years acct) to ye college by ye most reverd Dr most Revd Archbishop of Dublin and by his consent to be applyed to ye said buildings
rows.notes: Large narrative paragraph clearly visible; the amount 100.0.0 is clearly aligned at the far right. Description transcribed verbatim preserving archaic spelling. Very clear monetary column alignment.
Short 'Sum' label with total 305.17.5 clearly in right columns. Numeric alignment is crisp; validation: shillings 17 and pence 5 are within valid ranges.

The left label is clear. The numeric group looks like '901 01 05' but there is an extra small mark to the right that could be part of the pence or a scribble; interpreted as 901.1.5. Shillings and pence are in valid ranges but the stray mark reduces confidence.
Numeric '525.05.05' is reasonably clear in the columns. Handwriting of the label was legible and the numbers pass currency validation.
Amount 375.16.9 is legible and aligned to this Latin phrase. All numeric parts are within valid ranges.

rows.description: Sed petit allocari pro redundantibus in Cista
rows.notes: Label transcribed with some abbreviation expansion; numeric group reads '203.04.2' though final pence digit is small/faint. Values within valid ranges; moderate confidence due to faint pence.
Numeric cluster appears as '172.11.9' though there are stray marks near the pence; transcription chosen as the most consistent reading. Latin label clear.

rows.description: Sic remanent in Cista hoc Anno
rows.notes: This final 'remainder' total repeats a figure similar to earlier 'Sic debet' (375.16.9). The numeric group is visible but slightly smudged; values pass validation.
Clear header introducing the arrears list for the year 1700; no amounts directly on this header line.
Amount 60.0.0 clearly visible at far right of this arrears line. Description is brief but legible.

Amount reads as 28.07.03; the shillings/pence digits are slightly crowded but legible. Place-name 'Longwittenham' is a best reading of the script ('Longwittenham/Longwittenham'); moderate confidence on name spelling.
Amount 20.0.0 clearly visible. Place name 'Minhinnet' transcribed as appears; handwriting for name legible though archaic spelling preserved.
Amount 58.0.0 is clear. The place-name read as 'Southwinginton' from script; spelling reproduced as on page.
Amount 6.6.0 clearly visible. 'Merton pro anno' is legible and straightforward.
Amount 7.10.0 visible. The place-name is somewhat unclear in manuscript (read as 'Thirup') and the Latin/Italian term after it is abbreviated; reproduced literally with moderate confidence.
